title: zephyranthes jonesii
intro: Zephyranthes jonesii, also known as Jones' Zephyr Lily or White Wildflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It is native to the island of Hawaii and is known for its white, waxy flowers and grass-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Zephyranthes jonesii typically involve the root of the plant, which can be dried and made into a tea or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
